subject: UX & Research
title: Wayfinding Lessons from Airports
description: A quick, practical guide to Wayfinding Lessons from Airports.

hook.text: Airports guide millions of confused travelers daily. Their wayfinding systems are decades ahead of most websites.

buildup.text: Airport signs use progressive disclosure: first 'Departures,' then 'Terminal 2,' then 'Gates B1-B20,' then your gate.

discovery.text: Each sign gives just enough to take the next step. Users never need to process the full map at once.

twist.text: Bad airports overwhelm with signs pointing everywhere. Good ones reduce decisions to one at each junction.

climax.text: Apply airport logic to your site: one clear choice per junction, progressive detail, and consistent placement.

punchline.text: One choice per junction, just enough for the next step. Airport logic works.

quiz.question: What wayfinding principle do good airports apply that websites should copy?
options: Progressive disclosure — one clear choice per junction with just enough info for the next step
Showing the complete map at every location
Using color-coded sections throughout the entire facility
